headlines.title: Russian opposition leader jailed over anti-war social media posts

headlines.title: Venezuela earthquakes live: Buildings fall in Caracas, casualties feared
headlines.snippet: A powerful magnitude 7.5 earthquake struck Venezuela, with a precursor magnitude 7.1 quake also hitting the region. Buildings reportedly collapsed in the capital, Caracas, and casualties are feared.

headlines.title: Buildings collapse in Venezuelan capital after powerful earthquakes hit
headlines.snippet: Powerful earthquakes have caused buildings to collapse in Venezuela's capital. The exact number of casualties remains unknown but is feared to be high.

headlines.title: Two years on, Kenyan families still seek justice after Gen Z protests
headlines.snippet: Two years after Gen Z protests, families in Kenya are still seeking justice for those killed and missing. They state that investigations have stalled, and accountability has not been achieved.

headlines.title: Venezuela struck by back-to-back earthquakes, many casualties feared
headlines.snippet: Two strong earthquakes have struck Venezuela, raising fears of significant casualties. The US Geological Survey has indicated that high casualties are probable following the powerful tremors in the South American country.

headlines.title: Trump criticises European allies over lack of support during war on Iran
headlines.snippet: Donald Trump has criticized European allies for a perceived lack of support during the US-Israeli war on Iran. He stated that while the US did not require assistance, stronger backing from European governments would have been welcomed.

headlines.title: Trump casts doubt on US responsibility for deadly Iran school strike
headlines.snippet: Donald Trump stated he has seen no evidence suggesting the United States was responsible for a missile strike that hit an Iranian primary school. The incident occurred on February 28, the first day of the US-Israeli war on Iran.

headlines.title: Vance says US reviewing conditions for Türkiye's F-35 purchase
headlines.snippet: US Vice President JD Vance is reviewing the conditions for Turkey's purchase of F-35 fighter jets with the Pentagon. He stated that they are verifying if Turkey has met the legal requirements mandated by American law.

headlines.title: UN nuclear chief says inspections in Iran will go ahead despite Tehran's caution
headlines.snippet: The head of the IAEA stated that UN nuclear inspectors will visit Iran's enrichment sites. This will proceed despite Tehran's indication that inspections would only occur after a final agreement is reached.
